description_long: This object is a vintage portable radio receiver with a rectangular, boxy form and a sturdy, utilitarian design. The front panel features a horizontal frequency indicator with white lines and a pair of prominent round dials, likely for tuning and volume control, as well as several smaller knobs and switches for additional adjustments. Below the control panel, a fold-down cover or tray is present, possibly for protecting the controls or serving as a writing or storage surface. The top of the radio has a brown leather or faux-leather carrying handle, attached with metal brackets, making it easy to transport. The sides and back are plain, with a smooth, metallic or plastic finish, and the overall color scheme is a combination of light gray, dark gray, and black. The design suggests a mid-20th-century aesthetic, suitable for use in urban environments such as parks, garages, or street corners.
description: A vintage, boxy portable radio receiver with a front control panel, fold-down cover, and a brown carrying handle. The object features multiple knobs, a frequency indicator, and a sleek gray and black color scheme.
description_view_0: Front view showing the control panel with dials, switches, frequency indicator, and the fold-down cover extended.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the radio and the profile of the carrying handle, with the fold-down cover visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, smooth surface with the handle on top and no visible controls or openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other profile, again with the handle and the extended fold-down cover.
